threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still consider the bus as a backup plan instead of your primary ride. Locals know it’s all about the Overground.
Caught the 59 at 3 AM, a guy serenading the driver with '80s pop. Everyone joined in, even the grumpy bloke in the back. London nights, eh?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a guy in flip-flops arguing passionately with a street artist over the best currywurst in town. That’s Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do you find currywurst stands open at 3 AM, serving late-night students and artists who swear it’s the best cure for a hangover.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by how they curse under their breath at tourists blocking the sidewalk while snapping photos of murals.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a glimpse of a guy serenading commuters with a ukulele at Civic Center. Meanwhile, that dog on the tracks felt like a perfect metaphor for Muni's unpredictability.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think paella is everywhere, but in Gràcia, you’re more likely to find a great plate of escalivada than any rice dish.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a new arrival by their struggle with the metro ticket machine; it's like watching someone try to decipher hieroglyphics.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look confused when the bus driver doesn’t wait for you to sit down before pulling away.
Last night on the 333, a guy serenaded the whole bus with Whitney Houston while everyone else just stared. Only in Streatham, right?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I found a kebab shop open at 4 AM, with the owner chatting about politics while the late-night crowd lingered on the sidewalk.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain do you find a bar that serves cocktails exclusively made from pickled vegetables. I swear, it's a whole vibe!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by how they roll their eyes at the surprise of a BART delay—like, welcome to our Friday chaos. It’s just part of the routine.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the N-Judah powering through the Tenderloin, dodging a group of breakdancers taking over the station. Only in SF do you get a front-row seat to the show.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think we all sip wine at 2 AM. Most Gràcians are home by midnight—just trying to keep up with work and the kids.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a newcomer by their hesitation at crossing the street. They still expect cars to stop at red lights—good luck with that!
